summary.intent: Implement pass-8 review findings for the carousel component, fixing four correctness issues around state machine transitions, drag guards, keyboard navigation, and focus-based pause behavior.
summary.outcome: All four findings implemented and tested: auto-play timer re-armed on TransitionEnd, drag blocked during Transitioning state, End key mapped to max_start_index(), and FocusEnter event added to pause on control focus. 143 tests pass, clippy clean, spec synced, committed and pushed.

repo: Carousel lives in crates/ars-components/src/layout/carousel/mod.rs and has a companion spec at spec/components/layout/carousel.md that must be kept in sync with every implementation change.
The project uses a custom CI shortcut `cargo xci-fast` as the final gate before commit/push.
The carousel uses an explicit state machine with named states (AutoPlaying, Transitioning, etc.) and named events (FocusSlide, FocusEnter, PointerDown, PointerUp, TransitionEnd, etc.).
Codex is used as an async code-review oracle; results arrive via task notifications and are fetched in numbered 'passes' (pass-8 here).
Spec changes mirror implementation: event variants, transition arms, and handler prose all have corresponding spec sections (§1.2 events, transition table, §3.3 focus prose).

code.finding: TransitionEnd must re-arm the auto-play timer; a swipe leaves it cancelled so this was previously a silent bug.
PointerDown must be guarded to block drag initiation while in the Transitioning state to prevent overlapping animations.
PointerUp needs a defensive guard against triggering navigation when state is Transitioning, not just PointerDown.
End key must jump to max_start_index() rather than the last slide index to correctly handle multi-visible-slide carousels.

FocusEnter event variant added alongside on_root_focus_in/on_root_focus_out handlers, placed near on_root_pointer_enter for consistency. Pauses auto-play when any control (not just slides) receives focus.

workflow: Initial test written for PointerUp-during-transition was bogus and had to be rewritten; the correct approach constructs the scenario via PointerDown (drag recording) followed by GoToSlide to enter Transitioning.
Spec sync is a non-trivial step: events, transition arms, handler definitions, and prose sections (e.g. §3.3) each need independent updates.
Six new tests were added for pass-8 findings, bringing the total from 137 to 143.

friction: First attempt at the pointer_up_during_transition test was incorrect and required a rewrite to properly construct the Transitioning state scenario.
Spec sync required multiple targeted reads and edits across different sections (events, transition table, handlers, prose) with no single-pass update path.
